summary: Backend-only test pass for YLA Owner Memory + Stripe Checkout + admin auth + existing endpoints. Created /app/backend/tests/test_yla_backend.py with 36 pytest cases spanning admin auth (cookie + 401 gates), Owner Memory CRUD + length validation + ascending sort, owner chat auto-extraction (verified source='auto' facts appear within ~10s of owner chat with durable info), non-owner chat does NOT trigger extraction, Stripe checkout session creation for 'lifetime' + 'starter' with invalid-plan 400, status fetch updates transaction row, admin/payments listing confirms 'initiated' payment_transactions rows, plus regression on /access, /register-email, /admin/stats|users|reviews|mark-paid|grant-lifetime|revoke, /review validation, and anonymous /chat trial. 36/36 PASSED against public REACT_APP_BACKEND_URL in 21.58s.

minor.issue: Response does not strip Mongo _id via pydantic model but doc.pop('_id', None) is called post-insert; works correctly. Consider returning MemoryFact model for consistency.
No rate-limit / no auth; any anonymous caller can create Stripe sessions. Low risk given fixed server-side packages, but worth revisiting.
Not tested (requires real Stripe signature). Code path reviewed only.

critical_code_review_comments: server.py is 930 lines — approaching the 700-line guideline. Recommend splitting into routers: auth.py, admin.py, memory.py, payments.py, chat.py.

asyncio.create_task(extract_and_save_owner_facts(...)) is fire-and-forget with no reference stored; task can be garbage-collected mid-run on some event loops. Low-impact because OpenAI calls are short, but saving the task reference or using a background task queue is safer.
/api/payments/checkout/session is unauthenticated — anyone can create Stripe sessions tied to any session_id. Consider at least session-id ownership validation or basic rate limiting.
load_owner_memory() is called per chat turn to build the system prompt, which hits Mongo on every owner chat. Consider a simple in-process TTL cache invalidated on memory writes.
admin_login sets cookie with secure=True which is correct for https preview but will prevent local http dev; consider conditioning on env.
extract_and_save_owner_facts dedups by lower().strip() but does not enforce any similarity dedupe — near-duplicate auto facts can accumulate over time.
seed_data_creation: Test created TEST_ prefixed user_access rows (grant/revoke test), payment_transactions rows for lifetime+starter Stripe sessions (status='initiated', not paid), and transient owner_memory facts (cleaned up at session start+end by autouse fixture).
context_for_next_testing_agent: Regression suite lives at /app/backend/tests/test_yla_backend.py. Re-run with: pytest backend/tests/test_yla_backend.py -v --junitxml=/app/test_reports/pytest/pytest_results.xml. Owner auto-extraction waits up to 12s per poll loop — depends on OpenAI gpt-4o-mini latency. Stripe test uses sk_test_emergent and returns real checkout.stripe.com URLs (not paid in tests). Cleanup fixture clears owner_memory before+after; payment_transactions TEST_* rows are left behind (harmless).
rca of the issue: No issues found — all endpoints behaved per spec.
